summary: Blend a source image onto a destination through a coverage mask at an opacity, over a restricted exactly-pinned mode set (normal/over, add, subtract, multiply, screen, darken, lighten, difference) in premultiplied linear light; out = dst + opacity·mask·(B_mode(src, dst) − dst).

inputs.name: src
inputs.doc: The premultiplied linear-light source image being blended.

inputs.name: dst
inputs.doc: The premultiplied linear-light destination image blended onto.

inputs.name: mask
inputs.doc: The coverage mask in [0, 1] modulating the blend; a full (all-ones) mask blends everywhere.

outputs.name: image
outputs.doc: The blended image (the dst descriptor; same extent/layout).

params.name: mode
params.doc: The blend mode; one of the restricted exactly-pinned set.

params.name: opacity
params.doc: The global blend opacity in [0, 1]; 0 is the identity on dst.

perceptual.reason: composite.blend is a bit-exact per-channel blend over a restricted mode set verified by per-mode formula fixtures, opacity/mask identity, and commutativity properties; there is no perceptual-quality metric to apply
